mcp.title: MCP Server Integration
mcp.subtitle: Connect Tabularis to Claude Desktop, Cursor, and more
mcp.description: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) allows AI assistants (like Claude) to connect to your local tools. Tabularis exposes an MCP server that lets AI read your database schema and execute queries safely.
mcp.manualText: Add this to your client config file manually if automatic install fails.
mcp.successMsg: Configuration installed successfully for {{client}}! Restart the app to apply.
mcp.manualCommandText: Run this command in your terminal, then restart Claude Code.

connections.title: Connections
connections.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this connection?
connections.failConnect: Failed to connect to {{name}}. Please check your settings or ensuring the database is running.
connections.noConnectionsHint: Create your first connection to get started.

settings.title: Settings
settings.pageSizeDesc: Limits the number of rows fetched per query to prevent performance issues. Set to 0 to disable (not recommended).
settings.copyFormatDesc: Choose the default format when copying rows with Ctrl+C / Cmd+C.
settings.csvDelimiterDesc: Choose the default delimiter character used when copying or exporting rows as CSV.
settings.fontSizeDesc: Adjust the base font size used throughout the application (10-20px).
settings.fontPreviewText: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
settings.appearance_editorThemeDesc: Choose an independent theme for the SQL editor, or keep it in sync with the app theme.
settings.appearance_editorWordWrapDesc: Wrap long lines in the editor instead of scrolling horizontally.
settings.appearance_editorShowLineNumbersDesc: Display line numbers in the editor gutter.
settings.editConfigJsonDesc: Directly edit the raw configuration file. A restart is required to apply changes.

configJsonModal.title: config.json
configJsonModal.restartRequired: A restart is required to apply changes.
configJsonModal.restartMessage: The configuration file has been saved. Restart now to apply the changes?
configJsonModal.invalidJson: Invalid JSON — please fix errors before saving.

settings.languageDesc: Choose your preferred language. 'Auto' will use your system language.
settings.roadmapDesc: This project is a Work In Progress (WIP). Core features are stable, but we have big plans.
settings.supportDesc: If you like tabularis and want to see more features, consider supporting the project by contributing code, reporting bugs, or starring the repo.

ai.title: AI Configuration
ai.description: Configure AI providers to enable natural language to SQL generation. Keys are stored securely in your system's keychain.
ai.enableDesc: Show AI Assist and Explain buttons in the editor
ai.modelDesc: Select the model to be used for generation and explanation.
ai.codexOauthDesc: Use your existing Codex / ChatGPT sign-in instead of entering an API key manually. Tabularis will reuse the OAuth session managed by Codex CLI.
ai.codexOauthLaunch: Opened Codex login in Terminal. Finish the browser sign-in flow, then click refresh.
ai.keyStoredSecurely: API Key is stored securely in your system keychain. Setting a key here overrides the environment variable.
ai.fromEnvTooltip: This key is loaded from an environment variable
ai.envVariableDetected: An environment variable is present, but you can override it by setting a key above.
ai.resetKey: Delete custom key and revert to environment variable (if present)
ai.systemPromptDesc: Instructions for AI-powered SQL generation. Use {{SCHEMA}} as a placeholder for the database structure.
ai.explainPromptDesc: Instructions for AI-powered query explanation. Use {{LANGUAGE}} as a placeholder for the output language.
ai.cellnamePromptDesc: Customize instructions for AI notebook cell name generation. The cell content (SQL or Markdown) is sent as the user message.
ai.tabrenamePromptDesc: Customize instructions for AI query result tab name generation. The SQL query is sent as the user message.
ai.explainplanPromptDesc: Customize instructions for AI analysis of EXPLAIN query plans. Use {{LANGUAGE}} for the output language.
ai.ollamaNotDetected: Ollama not detected on port {{port}}. Is it running?
ai.modelNotFound: Model {{model}} not found in {{provider}}. It may not work correctly.
ai.endpointUrlDesc: The base URL of your OpenAI-compatible API. Examples: https://api.groq.com/openai/v1, http://localhost:8000/v1
ai.customOpenaiModelDesc: Enter the model name provided by your OpenAI-compatible service.
ai.customOpenaiModelHelp: Enter the exact model name for your OpenAI-compatible provider.

settings.openSourceLibrariesDesc: Browse the direct open source dependencies used across the app, backend, and tooling.
settings.openSourceLibrariesSource: Direct dependencies declared in package.json and src-tauri/Cargo.toml.
settings.autoCheckUpdatesDesc: Automatically check for new versions when the app launches
settings.enableLoggingDesc: Collect application logs in memory for debugging
settings.maxLogEntriesDesc: How many logs to keep in memory (1-10000)
settings.clearLogsConfirm: Are you sure you want to clear all logs?
settings.pingIntervalDesc: How often to check if active connections are still alive. Set to 0 to disable.
settings.queryHistoryMaxEntriesDesc: Maximum number of query history entries stored per connection.
settings.showWelcomeDesc: Display the welcome screen when the application starts.
settings.erDiagramDefaultLayoutDesc: Choose the default layout direction for ER diagrams

plugins.title: Plugins
plugins.availableDesc: Browse and install plugins from the registry.
plugins.noPlugins: No plugins available in the registry.
plugins.installedDesc: Enable or disable database drivers. Built-in drivers cannot be disabled.
plugins.confirmRemove: Are you sure you want to remove "{{name}}"? This will delete the plugin files.

installError.title: Installation Failed
installError.subtitle: An error occurred while installing the plugin. See the details below.

startError.title: Plugin Failed to Start
startError.subtitle: The plugin process could not be launched. Check the error details below.
startError.interpreterHint: This plugin may require an interpreter (e.g. Python). Use Plugin Settings to configure the interpreter path.

pluginSettings.title: Plugin Settings
pluginSettings.interpreterDesc: Optional. Specify the executable used to run this plugin (e.g. python3 on macOS/Linux, python or a full path on Windows). Leave blank to use the default.

maxAllowedPacket.label: Max Allowed Packet
maxAllowedPacket.description: Maximum packet size used by the MySQL connector.

timezone.label: Timezone
timezone.description: Session timezone sent to MySQL after connect.

shortcuts.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
shortcuts.modifierHintMac: Use ⌘ (Cmd) as the main modifier. All shortcuts can be customized.
shortcuts.modifierHintWin: Use Ctrl as the main modifier. All shortcuts can be customized.

update.installingMessage: The app will restart automatically after installation
update.managedByPackageManagerDesc: Use your package manager to update Tabularis.

ai.configRequired: ⚠️ AI Provider not configured. Please go to Settings > AI.

ai.promptPlaceholder: e.g. Find all users who signed up last month and ordered a 'Premium' plan...

editor.noActiveSession: No active session. Please select a connection.
editor.executePrompt: Execute a query to see results
editor.tableRunPrompt: Press Run (Ctrl/Command+F5) to load table data

visualExplain.title: Visual Explain
visualExplain.analyzeWarning: ANALYZE executes the query. Use with caution on data-modifying statements.
visualExplain.notSupported: EXPLAIN is not supported for this connection type.
visualExplain.notExplainable: EXPLAIN is only supported for DML statements (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE). DDL statements like CREATE, DROP, or ALTER cannot be explained.
visualExplain.selectNode: Select a node to view details
visualExplain.noIssues: No major issues detected in the current plan summary.
visualExplain.postgresAnalyzeLegend1: PostgreSQL ANALYZE includes actual rows, timing, loops, and buffer counters when available.
visualExplain.postgresAnalyzeLegend2: Large estimate gaps usually indicate stale statistics or predicates the planner cannot model well.
visualExplain.postgresEstimateLegend1: PostgreSQL without ANALYZE shows planner estimates only.
visualExplain.postgresEstimateLegend2: Enable ANALYZE to inspect actual rows, timing, loops, and buffers.
visualExplain.mysqlAnalyzeLegend1: MySQL and MariaDB expose actual metrics only on supported EXPLAIN ANALYZE or ANALYZE FORMAT variants.
visualExplain.mysqlAnalyzeLegend2: Older servers may fall back to estimated plans with fewer metrics.
visualExplain.mysqlEstimateLegend1: MySQL and MariaDB may fall back to EXPLAIN FORMAT=JSON or tabular EXPLAIN depending on server version.
visualExplain.mysqlEstimateLegend2: If timing is missing, the server likely returned an estimate-only plan.
visualExplain.sqliteLegend1: SQLite EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN is lightweight and mostly structural.
visualExplain.sqliteLegend2: Cost, timing, and row estimates are often unavailable compared with PostgreSQL and MySQL.
visualExplain.noRawOutput: No raw explain output available to export.
visualExplain.aiConfigRequired: AI Provider not configured. Please go to Settings > AI.

killModal.subtitle: This action cannot be undone immediately
killModal.descriptionAfter: will forcefully stop its process. Any active database connections using this plugin will stop working until the plugin is restarted.
killModal.warning: Active queries and connections through this plugin will be interrupted.
